w20: GPU-accelerated compute with 8 NVIDIA A100s and 680GB RAM, optimized for large-scale AI and data processing.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g: Accelerator-optimized instance with 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 vCPUs, and 680GB RAM for demanding AI workloads.
c200: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g offers extreme acceleration with 8 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPUs, 96 x86_64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 680GB memory, ideal for advanced machine learning and scientific computing.
w150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized instance featuring an x86_64 Intel Xeon architecture with 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores operating at 2.2 GHz. While single-core CPU performance is weak, multi-core CPU performance is strong, placing it in the top 25%. The server is equipped with 680 GB of RAM, demonstrating strong memory bandwidth for both small and large block read operations, and robust large block write performance. It bundles 8 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPUs, providing a substantial 320 GB of total VRAM. Local storage is not included. This configuration excels in LLM inference, achieving top-tier speeds across various model sizes for both prompt processing and text generation. It is well-suited for demanding data science jobs, large-scale machine learning training, and high-throughput LLM inference, where parallel processing and extensive GPU resources are critical.
